title: 2. Private Container Registries & Corporate Proxy Integration

blocks.content: Enterprise environments route all external traffic through forward proxies (such as Squid or Zscaler) that perform SSL interception. To deploy agy-box reliably, you must configure the container runtime to trust corporate certificates and route image pull and push requests through the appropriate proxies.

> Failing to inject corporate Root CA certificates into both the host container daemon and the agy-box image itself will result in immediate SSL handshake failures (x509: certificate signed by unknown authority) when communicating with public or private APIs.
To ensure the engine can download dependencies and connect to LLM endpoints, the enterprise Root CA certificate must be copied into the container during the build stage.
# Custom Dockerfile extending the base agy-box image
# Switch to root to import certificates
Configure the container engine (Podman/Docker) to use the corporate proxy for pulling images, while bypassing the proxy for internal private registries.
# Create systemd override directory for Podman/Docker
# Define proxy environment variables in systemd override configuration
# Reload systemd daemon and restart the container service
# Authenticate to the private registry using corporate credentials
Question 1: What is the primary cause of the error 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority' during an agy-box deploy run inside a corporate environment?
A) The agent API key is invalid.
B) The container runtime lacks the enterprise's custom SSL Root CA certificate in its trusted store.
C) The system timezone is out of sync with the cloud provider.
D) Podman is running in rootful instead of rootless mode.

> Correct Answer: B. Corporate proxies intercept SSL traffic and resign certificates. The container will reject these connection attempts unless the custom enterprise Root CA certificate is injected and trusted.
Question 2: How do you configure a container daemon to bypass corporate proxies for a local private registry?
A) By setting the proxy IP in HTTPS_PROXY.
B) By deleting the docker daemon configuration.
C) By adding the private registry hostname or IP to the NO_PROXY environment variable list.
D) By running the registry on port 80 without SSL.
> Correct Answer: C. The NO_PROXY environment variable lists hostnames and IP addresses that must be accessed directly, bypassing the proxy.
